title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
summary: This is a discussion between Dwarkesh Patel and Ada Palmer about the role of Renaissance art as a form of social or political influence. The framing presents art as an instrumental tool rather than purely aesthetic.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel talks with historian Ada Palmer about Renaissance art treated as an instrument of social and political influence rather than a purely aesthetic object. The framing reframes familiar works as tools deployed for persuasion and power. The takeaway is a view of Renaissance art as a weapon, shifting the question from what the art looks like to what it was meant to accomplish.
